title: Getting started with cleanplots
text: want cleanplots colors – leaving theme, sizes, everything else exactly – add scale_color_cleanplots() (scale_fill_cleanplots()) plot, just like palette package:  One recommendation: using full cleanplots setup (theme_cleanplots() cleanplots_defaults(), ), add theme_minimal() plots – colors markers much easier see white background ggplot2’s default gray. examples vignette . scales take options: palette: "default" (main colors) "bars" (softer versions bars, areas, pies – ) order: use colors different order, e.g. order = c(7, 1, 2) starts navy reverse: reverse palette  use individual colors manually, cleanplots_colors() returns hex codes name:
code: ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width, color = Species)) +   geom_point() +   scale_color_cleanplots() +   theme_minimal() ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width, color = Species)) +   geom_point() +   scale_color_cleanplots(order = c(7, 1, 2)) +   theme_minimal() cleanplots_colors() #>       red    ltblue     black      gray    purple      pink      navy    ltgray  #> "#D50000" "#8FC6EB" "#000000" "#909090" "#740074" "#FFB3D9" "#143755" "#C0C0C0"  #>    dkgray  lavender  #> "#404040" "#D9D7F0" cleanplots_colors("red", "navy") #>       red      navy  #> "#D50000" "#143755"

title: Getting started with cleanplots
text: main palette used markers, lines, confidence intervals. alternates darker lighter colors first several groups remain distinguishable even printed black & white, contains red–green pair (red-green colorblindness common form, affecting roughly 8% men). bar palette contains softer versions colors. Bars, areas, pie slices use far ink points lines, full-strength colors overwhelm; cleanplots uses gentler fills elements:
code: titanic <- aggregate(Freq ~ Class + Sex, data = as.data.frame(Titanic), sum)  ggplot(titanic, aes(Sex, Freq, fill = Class)) +   geom_col(position = "dodge") +   scale_fill_cleanplots(palette = "bars") +   labs(x = NULL, y = "Passengers and crew", fill = "Class") +   theme_minimal()

title: Getting started with cleanplots
text: Color alone reliably distinguishes 4–5 groups. Beyond – black & white printing colorblind readers – cleanplots varies multiple visual channels , group pair ever depends single cue: Color lightness: palette alternates dark light. Marker shape fill: scale_shape_cleanplots() gives hollow shapes (circle, square, triangle, diamond) dark colors solid shapes light colors. Line pattern: scale_linetype_cleanplots() assigns solid, solid, dashed, dashed, shortdash, shortdash, longdash, longdash. assignments coordinated: two groups share shape line pattern always differ strongly lightness, every pair groups separated least two independent channels.
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy, color = class, shape = class)) +   geom_point(size = 2, stroke = 0.7) +   scale_color_cleanplots() +   scale_shape_cleanplots() +   theme_minimal() ggplot(economics_long, aes(date, value01, color = variable, linetype = variable)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.75) +   scale_color_cleanplots() +   scale_linetype_cleanplots() +   theme_minimal()

title: Getting started with cleanplots
text: theme_cleanplots() applies cleanplots layout: white background, plot border, light gray axis lines, dotted gridlines, frameless legend right, black-outlined facet strips. Like ggplot2 theme, add per plot:
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy, color = class, shape = class)) +   geom_point(size = 2, stroke = 0.7) +   scale_color_cleanplots() +   scale_shape_cleanplots() +   theme_cleanplots()

title: Getting started with cleanplots
text: Adding scales themes every plot gets repetitive. One call top script applies complete cleanplots setup session: , scales theme added: every plot uses theme_cleanplots(); discrete color aesthetics use main palette, discrete fill aesthetics use softer bar palette (bars areas automatically get gentler colors); points larger heavier outlines (size = 2, stroke = 0.7), hollow marker shapes clearly visible; lines thicker (linewidth = 0.75) geom_line(), geom_path(), geom_step(), geom_density(), geom_function(), geom_smooth(); error bars, linereanges, pointranges use 80% ; geom_smooth() fit lines cleanplots red light gray confidence band, instead ggplot2’s blue.   settings arguments prefer different sizes: default text size (12) chosen match body text academic article figure saved recommended size (see next section): can read article, can read graph. contexts need bigger text – presentations lectures especially – increase : Everything remains overridable per plot: explicit scale, theme, aesthetic always wins. example, use main palette fill instead automatic bar palette:  restore ggplot2’s defaults: cleanplots_defaults() sets session-wide state, persists restart R. reset manually:
code: cleanplots_defaults() ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy, color = class, shape = class)) +   geom_point() +   scale_shape_cleanplots() ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy)) +   geom_point(color = cleanplots_colors("gray")) +   geom_smooth(method = "loess", formula = y ~ x) cleanplots_defaults(base_size = 12, point_size = 2,                     point_stroke = 0.7, line_width = 0.75,                     smooth_color = "#D50000") cleanplots_defaults(base_size = 16)   # presentations and lectures ggplot(titanic, aes(Sex, Freq, fill = Class)) +   geom_col(position = "dodge") +   scale_fill_cleanplots(palette = "default") +   labs(x = NULL, y = "Passengers and crew", fill = "Class") theme_set(theme_gray()) options(ggplot2.discrete.colour = NULL, ggplot2.discrete.fill = NULL) update_geom_defaults("point", list(size = 1.5, stroke = 0.5))

title: Getting started with cleanplots
text: important quirk ggplot2: plot intrinsic size. Text, markers, lines physical units (points millimeters), ggsave() without explicit dimensions saves whatever size plot window happens – code produces figures different proportions different days, machines, window layouts. fix always save explicit dimensions. cleanplots_save() tuned defaults – 7 x 5 inches 300 dpi: 7 x 5? matches default figure size R Markdown (HTML) documents, 7-inch figure placed 6.5-inch text width US-letter manuscript scales 12-point default text 11 points – comparable article’s body text. file extension sets format, .pdf, .tiff, .eps journal submission systems work directly. Override default needed: principle applies ways saving: ggsave() directly: always pass width, height, dpi. RStudio’s Export button: type explicit dimensions dialog (e.g., 2100 x 1500 pixels = 7 x 5 inches 300 dpi) rather accepting window size. R Markdown / Quarto: figures sized chunk options, already fixed-size construction. match cleanplots_save(): knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.width = 7, fig.height = 5) (fig-width/fig-height Quarto). Note pdf_document defaults 6.5 x 4.5 – article text width – also works well. Base devices (png() … print(p) … dev.()): pass dimensions device call. Avoid copy-pasting figures plot window Word PowerPoint: result window-sized screen-resolution.
code: p <- ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy, color = drv)) + geom_point() cleanplots_save("my-figure.png", p) cleanplots_save("slide-figure.png", p, width = 10, height = 5.6)

what: 7. Plots of predictions and marginal effects
title: Getting started with cleanplots
text: cleanplots works box marginaleffects modelsummary packages. cleanplots_defaults() set, plots pick colors theme automatically. examples use data life satisfaction across ages: coefficient plot nested models modelsummary::modelplot():  Adjusted predictions nominal predictor marginaleffects::plot_predictions():  Predictions across continuous predictor group. One note : plot_predictions() draws confidence-interval ribbons heavy transparency (alpha = 0.1), tuned saturated colors. Add scale_fill_cleanplots(palette = "default") ribbons use full-strength palette rather soft bar colors:  full control ribbons (anything else), use plot_predictions(..., draw = FALSE) get plotting data build figure :  comparisons – , marriage gap life satisfaction across ages – marginaleffects::plot_comparisons():
code: sim <- readRDS(url(   "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tdmize/data/master/data/sim_lifesat.rds",   open = "rb")) mod1 <- lm(lifesat ~ age + educ, data = sim) mod2 <- lm(lifesat ~ age + educ + married, data = sim)  modelsummary::modelplot(list("Baseline" = mod1, "+ Marriage" = mod2),                         coef_omit = "Intercept") +   geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +   labs(x = "Coefficient estimates and 95% confidence intervals") mod <- lm(lifesat ~ poly(age, 2) * married + educ, data = sim)  marginaleffects::plot_predictions(mod, condition = c("educ", "married")) +   labs(y = "Predicted life satisfaction") marginaleffects::plot_predictions(mod, condition = c("age", "married")) +   scale_fill_cleanplots(palette = "default") +   labs(y = "Predicted life satisfaction") pr <- marginaleffects::plot_predictions(   mod, condition = c("age", "married"), draw = FALSE)  ggplot(pr, aes(age, estimate, color = married, fill = married)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),               alpha = .35, color = NA) +   geom_line() +   labs(y = "Predicted life satisfaction") marginaleffects::plot_comparisons(mod, variables = "married",                                   condition = "age") +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +   labs(y = "Difference in predicted life satisfaction\n(Married - Not married)")

title: Getting started with cleanplots
text: companion cleanplots scheme Stata shares package’s colors, marker symbols, line patterns, layout – hex values languages computed definitions, colors match exactly. mapping: Stata scheme available https://www.trentonmize.com/software/cleanplots.

what: cleanplots color scales and theme for ggplot2
title: cleanplots color scales and theme for ggplot2
text: Full documentation examples: tdmize.github.io/cleanplots Publication-ready defaults ggplot2: cleanplots graphing scheme. cleanplots provides professional-looking figures strong data visualization accessibility defaults – colors chosen colorblind-friendly remain distinguishable printed black & white, matching marker shapes line patterns groups stay distinct across multiple visual channels. companion scheme available Stata, figures made R Stata share design.

title: cleanplots color scales and theme for ggplot2
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("tdmize/cleanplots")

title: cleanplots color scales and theme for ggplot2
text: quickest way use cleanplots one setup call: sets theme, makes markers lines larger visible, applies cleanplots colors plots default (main palette color, softer bar palette fill): apply pieces individually per plot. (using full cleanplots setup, recommend adding theme_minimal() theme_cleanplots() – colors markers much easier see white background.)
code: library(ggplot2) library(cleanplots) cleanplots_defaults()  ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy, color = class, shape = class)) +   geom_point() +   scale_shape_cleanplots()  cleanplots_save("my-figure.png")   # saves at a fixed 7 x 5 in, 300 dpi library(ggplot2) library(cleanplots)  # Scatterplot with the main palette and theme ggplot(iris, aes(x = Sepal.Length, y = Sepal.Width, color = Species)) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   theme_cleanplots() +   scale_color_cleanplots()  # Bar charts use the softer bar/area palette titanic <- aggregate(Freq ~ Class + Sex, data = as.data.frame(Titanic), sum) ggplot(titanic, aes(Sex, Freq, fill = Class)) +   geom_col(position = "dodge") +   theme_cleanplots() +   scale_fill_cleanplots(palette = "bars")  # Reorder or reverse the colors scale_color_cleanplots(order = c(7, 1, 2)) scale_color_cleanplots(reverse = TRUE)  # Extract hex codes directly cleanplots_colors() cleanplots_colors("red", "navy") cleanplots_colors(bars = TRUE)

title: cleanplots color scales and theme for ggplot2
text: Main colors (palette = "default") – used markers, lines, confidence intervals: Bar/area colors (palette = "bars") – softer versions used bar charts, area plots, pie charts, use far ink points lines:

title: cleanplots color scales and theme for ggplot2
text: Color alone reliably distinguishes 4-5 groups. Beyond – black & white printing colorblind readers – cleanplots varies several visual channels : scale_shape_cleanplots() assigns hollow marker shapes dark colors solid shapes light colors, scale_linetype_cleanplots() assigns line patterns (solid, solid, dashed, dashed, shortdash, shortdash, longdash, longdash) two groups sharing shape pattern always differ strongly lightness. Groups remain distinguishable color, lightness, marker shape fill, line pattern.
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy, color = class, shape = class)) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   theme_cleanplots() +   scale_color_cleanplots() +   scale_shape_cleanplots()

title: cleanplots color scales and theme for ggplot2
text: palette alternates dark light colors first several groups distinguishable lightness alone printed black & white, contains red-green pair, passes deuteranopia, protanopia, tritanopia simulation checks first five colors (minimum Delta-E 21). Check palette Coloring Colorblindness.

title: cleanplots color scales and theme for ggplot2
text: theme_cleanplots(): white background, plot border, light gray axis lines ticks, dotted light gray gridlines, frameless, untitled legend right, black-outlined facet strips bold labels. restore legend title:
code: theme_cleanplots() + theme(legend.title = element_text())

title: cleanplots color scales and theme for ggplot2
text: original Stata scheme available https://www.trentonmize.com/software/cleanplots via net install cleanplots, ("https://tdmize.github.io/data/cleanplots") replace. Colors, marker symbols, line patterns, layout match package, figures made R Stata look like siblings.

what: Extract cleanplots colors as hex codes — cleanplots_colors
title: Extract cleanplots colors as hex codes — cleanplots_colors
text: Get hex color codes used cleanplots color scheme, either full palette specific colors name.

title: Extract cleanplots colors as hex codes — cleanplots_colors
code: cleanplots_colors(..., bars = FALSE)

title: Extract cleanplots colors as hex codes — cleanplots_colors
text: ... Character names colors return. none specified, colors palette returned. Available names: "red", "ltblue", "black", "gray", "purple", "pink", "navy", "ltgray", "dkgray", "lavender". bars Logical. TRUE, return softer bar/area versions colors (colors Stata's cleanplots scheme uses bar charts, area plots, pie charts). Default FALSE (main colors used markers lines).

title: Extract cleanplots colors as hex codes — cleanplots_colors
text: named character vector hex color codes.

title: Extract cleanplots colors as hex codes — cleanplots_colors
text: Mize, T. D. cleanplots: Stata graphing scheme. https://www.trentonmize.com/software/cleanplots

title: Extract cleanplots colors as hex codes — cleanplots_colors
code: cleanplots_colors() #>       red    ltblue     black      gray    purple      pink      navy    ltgray  #> "#D50000" "#8FC6EB" "#000000" "#909090" "#740074" "#FFB3D9" "#143755" "#C0C0C0"  #>    dkgray  lavender  #> "#404040" "#D9D7F0"   cleanplots_colors("red", "navy") #>       red      navy  #> "#D50000" "#143755"   cleanplots_colors(bars = TRUE) #>       red    ltblue     black      gray    purple      pink      navy    ltgray  #> "#FF4D4D" "#C2E0F5" "#5F5F5F" "#B1B1B1" "#AF5FAF" "#FFDBEE" "#5D7A93" "#D3D3D3"  #>    dkgray  lavender  #> "#909090" "#E8E7F6"

what: Set cleanplots defaults for the session — cleanplots_defaults
title: Set cleanplots defaults for the session — cleanplots_defaults
text: One call makes ggplot2 behave like Stata cleanplots scheme rest session: sets theme_cleanplots() default theme, makes points, markers, lines larger thicker (matching scheme's visible defaults), registers cleanplots color fill scales session defaults plots colored correctly without adding scales hand.

title: Set cleanplots defaults for the session — cleanplots_defaults
code: cleanplots_defaults(   base_size = 12,   point_size = 2,   point_stroke = 0.7,   line_width = 0.75,   smooth_color = "#D50000" )

title: Set cleanplots defaults for the session — cleanplots_defaults
text: base_size Base font size points passed theme_cleanplots() (default: 12). point_size Default size geom_point() markers (default: 2; ggplot2's default 1.5). point_stroke Default outline thickness markers, controls visible hollow shapes (default: 0.7; ggplot2's default 0.5). line_width Default line width geom_line(), geom_path(), geom_step(), geom_density(), geom_function(), geom_smooth() (default: 0.75; ggplot2's default 0.5). Error bars, line ranges, point ranges set 80% value. Reference lines (geom_hline(), geom_vline(), geom_abline()) deliberately left thin unobtrusive. smooth_color Default line color geom_smooth() (default: cleanplots red, "#D50000").

title: Set cleanplots defaults for the session — cleanplots_defaults
text: Invisibly returns NULL; called side effects.

title: Set cleanplots defaults for the session — cleanplots_defaults
text: calling cleanplots_defaults(): plots use theme_cleanplots() unless another theme added. geom_point() draws larger markers heavier outline (important hollow marker shapes). geom_line() friends draw thicker lines colors visible. geom_smooth() fit lines default cleanplots red light gray confidence band (instead ggplot2's blue). Discrete color aesthetics default main cleanplots palette, discrete fill aesthetics default softer bar palette (Stata, bars areas automatically use softer colors). Add scale explicitly override, e.g. scale_fill_cleanplots(palette = "default"). session-wide settings affect subsequent ggplot2 plots. undo , restart R call ggplot2::theme_set(ggplot2::theme_gray()), ggplot2::update_geom_defaults(), options(ggplot2.discrete.colour = NULL, ggplot2.discrete.fill = NULL).

title: Set cleanplots defaults for the session — cleanplots_defaults
code: library(ggplot2) cleanplots_defaults()  # No scales or theme needed -- everything is applied by default ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width, color = Species)) +   geom_point()

what: Save a plot at cleanplots' recommended size — cleanplots_save
title: Save a plot at cleanplots' recommended size — cleanplots_save
text: drop-replacement ggplot2::ggsave() sensible fixed defaults: 7 x 5 inches 300 dpi. ggsave() without explicit dimensions saves current size plot window, figures come different size every time window changes – text, marker, line sizes physical units, plot can look cramped sparse depending nothing window. cleanplots_save() gives every figure canvas.

title: Save a plot at cleanplots' recommended size — cleanplots_save
code: cleanplots_save(   filename,   plot = ggplot2::last_plot(),   width = 7,   height = 5,   dpi = 300,   ... )

title: Save a plot at cleanplots' recommended size — cleanplots_save
text: filename File name write . extension determines format. plot Plot save (default: last plot displayed). width, height Plot dimensions inches (default: 7 x 5). dpi Resolution raster formats (default: 300, print-quality). ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::ggsave() (e.g., bg, units, device).

title: Save a plot at cleanplots' recommended size — cleanplots_save
text: Invisibly returns file name.

title: Save a plot at cleanplots' recommended size — cleanplots_save
text: defaults chosen academic work: 7 x 5 inches matches default figure size R Markdown (HTML) documents, 7-inch-wide figure placed 6.5-inch text width US-letter manuscript scales default 12-point text 11 points – comparable article's body text. output format inferred file extension (.png, .pdf, .tiff, .eps, ...), journal submission formats work directly.

title: Save a plot at cleanplots' recommended size — cleanplots_save
code: library(ggplot2) p <- ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width, color = Species)) +   geom_point() +   scale_color_cleanplots() +   theme_cleanplots()  f <- tempfile(fileext = ".png") cleanplots_save(f, p)  # Override any default as needed, e.g. for presentations and lectures: cleanplots_save(f, p, width = 10, height = 5.6)

title: cleanplots color palette — palette_cleanplots
text: Generate palette function cleanplots color scheme. function usually called directly, within scale_color_cleanplots().

title: cleanplots color palette — palette_cleanplots
code: palette_cleanplots(palette = "default", reverse = FALSE, order = 1:10, ...)

title: cleanplots color palette — palette_cleanplots
text: palette Character name palette. One "default" (main colors used markers, lines, confidence intervals Stata cleanplots scheme) "bars" (softer, lighter versions scheme uses bar charts, area plots, pie charts). reverse Logical. palette reversed? order vector numbers 1 10 indicating order colors use (default: 1:10). ... Ignored (included compatibility).

title: cleanplots color palette — palette_cleanplots
text: function takes single integer n returns character vector n hex colors.

title: cleanplots color palette — palette_cleanplots
code: palette_cleanplots()(4) #> [1] "#D50000" "#8FC6EB" "#000000" "#909090"  palette_cleanplots("bars")(3) #> [1] "#FF4D4D" "#C2E0F5" "#5F5F5F"

what: cleanplots color scales for ggplot2 — scale_color_cleanplots
title: cleanplots color scales for ggplot2 — scale_color_cleanplots
text: Discrete color fill scales using cleanplots color scheme (Mize), port colors Stata cleanplots graphing scheme. palette designed clean, publication-ready plots predictions marginal effects.

title: cleanplots color scales for ggplot2 — scale_color_cleanplots
code: scale_color_cleanplots(   palette = "default",   reverse = FALSE,   order = 1:10,   aesthetics = "color",   ... )  scale_colour_cleanplots(   palette = "default",   reverse = FALSE,   order = 1:10,   aesthetics = "color",   ... )  scale_fill_cleanplots(   palette = "default",   reverse = FALSE,   order = 1:10,   aesthetics = "fill",   ... )

title: cleanplots color scales for ggplot2 — scale_color_cleanplots
text: palette Character name palette: "default" "bars". reverse Logical. palette reversed? order vector numbers 1 10 indicating order colors use (default: 1:10). aesthetics vector aesthetic names scale apply (e.g., "color" "fill"). ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::discrete_scale().

title: cleanplots color scales for ggplot2 — scale_color_cleanplots
text: "default" palette contains 10 colors used markers, lines, confidence intervals. "bars" palette contains softer, lighter versions colors Stata scheme uses bar charts, area plots, pie charts (elements use much ink); typically paired scale_fill_cleanplots(). cleanplots palette available discrete palette.

title: cleanplots color scales for ggplot2 — scale_color_cleanplots
text: Mize, T. D. cleanplots: Stata graphing scheme. https://www.trentonmize.com/software/cleanplots

title: cleanplots color scales for ggplot2 — scale_color_cleanplots
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot(iris, aes(x = Sepal.Length, y = Sepal.Width, color = Species)) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   theme_cleanplots() +   scale_color_cleanplots()   ggplot(iris, aes(x = Species, y = Sepal.Length, fill = Species)) +   geom_boxplot() +   theme_cleanplots() +   scale_fill_cleanplots(palette = "bars")   # Use colors in a different order ggplot(iris, aes(x = Sepal.Length, y = Sepal.Width, color = Species)) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   theme_cleanplots() +   scale_color_cleanplots(order = c(7, 1, 2))

what: cleanplots shape and linetype scales for ggplot2 — scale_shape_cleanplots
title: cleanplots shape and linetype scales for ggplot2 — scale_shape_cleanplots
text: Discrete shape linetype scales matching marker symbols line patterns Stata cleanplots scheme. Combined scale_color_cleanplots(), make series distinguishable even printed black & white viewed colorblindness: markers alternate hollow (dark colors) solid (light colors) shapes, series share line pattern always differ strongly lightness.

title: cleanplots shape and linetype scales for ggplot2 — scale_shape_cleanplots
code: scale_shape_cleanplots(...)  scale_linetype_cleanplots(...)

title: cleanplots shape and linetype scales for ggplot2 — scale_shape_cleanplots
text: ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::scale_shape_manual() ggplot2::scale_linetype_manual().

title: cleanplots shape and linetype scales for ggplot2 — scale_shape_cleanplots
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width,                  color = Species, shape = Species)) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   theme_cleanplots() +   scale_color_cleanplots() +   scale_shape_cleanplots()   ggplot(economics_long, aes(date, value01,                            color = variable, linetype = variable)) +   geom_line() +   theme_cleanplots() +   scale_color_cleanplots() +   scale_linetype_cleanplots()

title: cleanplots theme for ggplot2 — theme_cleanplots
text: ggplot2 theme replicates non-color settings Stata cleanplots graphing scheme: white background, plot-region border, light gray (gs10) axis lines ticks, dotted light gray horizontal vertical gridlines, frameless legend lower right.

title: cleanplots theme for ggplot2 — theme_cleanplots
code: theme_cleanplots(base_size = 12, base_family = "", legend_position = "right")

title: cleanplots theme for ggplot2 — theme_cleanplots
text: base_size Base font size points (default: 12, sized match body text academic article figure saved 7 x 5 inches; see cleanplots_save()). base_family Base font family (default: ""). legend_position Position legend (default: "right", vertically centered). Set "none" remove, value accepted ggplot2::theme(legend.position = ...).

title: cleanplots theme for ggplot2 — theme_cleanplots
text: ggplot2 theme object.

title: cleanplots theme for ggplot2 — theme_cleanplots
text: Correspondence Stata scheme file: White background (color background white) plot region border (linestyle plotregion none) Axis lines, ticks gs10 gray (color axisline gs10, etc.) Dotted gs10 major gridlines, horizontal vertical (draw_major_hgrid yes, draw_major_vgrid yes, linepattern major_grid dot, color major_grid gs10) minor gridlines Legend right, single column, frame, title (clockdir legend_position 3, numstyle legend_cols 1, linestyle legend none) Horizontal y-axis tick labels (anglestyle vertical_tick horizontal) Facet strips: black-outlined, unfilled boxes bold labels

title: cleanplots theme for ggplot2 — theme_cleanplots
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot(iris, aes(x = Sepal.Length, y = Sepal.Width, color = Species)) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   theme_cleanplots() +   scale_color_cleanplots()

title: cleanplots 0.2.0
text: Initial release. cleanplots_defaults(): one-call session setup – default theme, larger markers thicker lines, cleanplots colors applied plots automatically, geom_smooth() fit lines cleanplots red. theme_cleanplots(): facet strips drawn black-outlined, unfilled boxes bold labels; legend right, vertically centered; default text size 12 (sized match article body text recommended figure size). cleanplots_save(): save figures fixed 7 x 5 inches, 300 dpi, plots look regardless plot window. Discrete color fill scales (scale_color_cleanplots(), scale_fill_cleanplots()) main bar/area palettes. Shape linetype scales (scale_shape_cleanplots(), scale_linetype_cleanplots()) matching Stata scheme’s marker symbols line patterns. theme_cleanplots() replicating layout defaults Stata cleanplots graphing scheme (v1.0.4). cleanplots_colors() palette_cleanplots() extract hex codes.
